CONTENT.Honours: GuingampCoupe de la Ligue runner-up: 2018–19NantesCoupe de France: 2021–22
CONTENT.Club career: Coco is a youth exponent from EA Guingamp. He made his Ligue 1 debut on 1 February 2015 against Girondins de Bordeaux starting the match and being substituted after 60 minutes for Lionel Mathis.He made his 10th appearance in the 2016–17 season by scoring a brace in a 1–3 away win over Lyon on 22 October 2016.In July 2019, following Guingamp's relegation from Ligue 1, Coco joined FC Nantes.
CONTENT.External links: Marcus Coco at the French Football Federation (in French)Marcus Coco at the French Football Federation (archived) (in French)Marcus Coco – UEFA competition record (archive)
CONTENT.International career: In October 2023, Coco was called up to the Guadeloupe national team for a set of 2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League matches. He debuted in a 2–1 loss to Saint Lucia on 12 October 2023.

SUMMARY: Marcus Regis Coco (born 24 June 1996) is a Guadeloupean professional footballer who plays as a right midfielder, right wing-back  or right winger for Ligue 1 club Nantes and the Guadeloupe national team.
